image_prompt: SVG: side-view pair of yoked oxen pulling a plough across a ploughed field with farmer behind; dark/light brown oxen with white horns and humps; rural scene with sky and hills.
SVG: white light entering a glass prism on dark background, dispersing into visible spectrum, violet at bottom; labels for Red and Violet.

render_data.title: Dispersion of white light through a prism

image_prompt: SVG: Bohr-style nitrogen-14 atom with 7 red protons + 7 grey neutrons in nucleus; two electron shells (2 inner, 5 outer); legend.

SVG: atom with gamma ray emerging from nucleus (purple) and X-ray emerging from inner electron orbit (orange); key with two drop zones; draggable items Gamma ray / X-ray / Cosmic ray / Ultrasound wave.

render_data.title: Classify each type of radiation by where it is produced
render_data.prompt: Place each type of radiation into the correct category.

image_prompt: SVG: vertical sealed cylinder with piston compressing trapped gas; stacked masses on top; downward force arrow; volume scale on right; labels for area A = 9.0e-4 m² and p_atm = 100000 Pa.

render_data.title: Piston compressing a fixed mass of gas (Boyle's law)
render_data.vessel: vertical sealed cylinder with a piston
render_data.coupling: Total pressure P = P_atm + m·g/A; at m = 1.8 kg, P = 100000 + 19620 = 119620 Pa ≈ 1.20 × 10⁵ Pa.

image_prompt: SVG: two graphs — left Volume / cm³ (0–160) vs Pressure / kPa (0–450) as an inverse smooth curve through data points giving pV ≈ 11000; right 1/V × 10⁻³ / cm⁻³ (0–40) vs Pressure / kPa (0–450) as a straight line through the origin.
SVG: pV / kPa cm³ (1000–19000) vs Pressure / kPa (0–450), a near-horizontal best-fit line at ≈ 11000 with scattered data points so that V(75 kPa) = 11000/75 ≈ 147 cm³.
SVG: orange inflated balloon with knot and string, tape measure wrapped around it labelled 'Circumference'.
SVG: inflated balloon with straw attached to a horizontal string between two stands; nozzle on left with escaping-air arrow.

render_data.title: Balloon rocket released along a string (Newton's 2nd & 3rd laws)

image_prompt: SVG: three Distance / m (0–6) graphs of the v1 balloon data (28.5/0.75, 33.0/1.15, 36.4/1.48, 44.2/2.85, 51.7/4.35, 55.3/5.72) — vs Circumference / cm (25–60) rising curve; vs Circumference cubed / cm³ (0–200000) straight line through origin; vs 1/circumference / cm⁻¹ (0.018–0.036) falling curve.
SVG: sine waveform, Position / cm (−1.5 to 1.5) vs Time / s (0–4.5), period T = 2.0 s so frequency f = 0.50 Hz (~2.25 cycles shown).
SVG: four waveform options A–D of different frequencies; option C has period T = 2.0 s to match part (a) (0.50 Hz). A long period (~6 s), B high frequency (~0.8 s), D on a 0–1 s axis (~0.4 s).

render_data.title: Select the waveform with the same frequency (0.50 Hz)
render_data.task: Select the waveform whose frequency matches the wave in part (a): T = 2.0 s, f = 0.50 Hz.

image_prompt: SVG: Khalid (left) and Sena (right) with mobile phones; two cell towers in between with antennas; dashed radio waves between people and towers; fibre-optic link between towers.

render_data.title: Order the steps: how the phone network carries the call
render_data.prompt: Put the statements in order to outline how the phone network works (Khalid → Sena).
items: Khalid's voice is converted into an electrical signal, then into a radio wave by his phone
The radio wave is transmitted from Khalid's phone to tower A
Tower A sends the signal to tower B through fibre-optic cables, where it is converted back into a radio wave
Sena's phone receives the radio wave and converts it back into sound she can hear
correctOrder: Khalid's voice is converted into an electrical signal, then into a radio wave by his phone
The radio wave is transmitted from Khalid's phone to tower A
Tower A sends the signal to tower B through fibre-optic cables, where it is converted back into a radio wave
Sena's phone receives the radio wave and converts it back into sound she can hear
